assumptions: Scope is app/engine/ top-level C-series clusters C1-C8; C2/C5 (turn-row creation, onboarding state machines) are the riskiest and get the most review attention.
Where two drivers intentionally diverge (sequential vs simultaneous), only the genuinely identical primitives are unified; deliberate differences are preserved and noted.
Reviews are run by independent Claude sub-agents acting adversarially, since Codex/Gemini are not installed.
discovery.summary: Behavior-preserving dedup of the app/engine C-series (C1-C8) from the duplication inventory: unify the two turn drivers' shared primitives, the is-bot predicate, active-player-count, onboarding state machines + _has_moved, liveness-window check, redundant standings sort, and match-cancel transition behind single-source helpers, with full Preflight Gate and tests.
non_goals: Any functional or behavioral change to gameplay, scheduling timing, or APIs.
The non-engine inventory items (A1 datetime sweep, B3/B4 route helpers, F2/F3) — deferred to a separate effort.
Cluster C7's documented-intentional dict-vs-schema scoreboard pair (only the genuinely redundant re-inlined sort is in scope).
acceptance_criteria: Each addressed C-series cluster has its duplicated logic collapsed to one shared definition with all prior call sites delegating to it.

No behavior change: identical turn sequencing, deadlines, submission/message counting, bot detection, onboarding-state transitions, liveness windows, standings ordering, and match-cancel side effects.
Full Preflight Gate green: ruff check . && mypy app/ mcp_server/ && pytest -q (1291+ tests).
New/updated tests lock in the unified helpers' behavior where engine logic is touched (per CLAUDE.md testing rules).

checklist.goal: Eliminate the C-series accidental code duplication in app/engine/ identified by the duplication inventory, with zero behavior change, so the two turn drivers and the connection/onboarding code share single-source helpers instead of copied logic.
checklist.audience: Maintainers of the hoard-hurt-help game engine (Claude/Codex/Gemini agents and Chris).
checklist.constraints: Behavior-preserving only; no suppressions (no # type: ignore/# noqa); fail-loud; async consistency; no vague filenames; one feature per branch; Preflight Gate before push. Codex/Gemini CLIs are unavailable in this environment, so all FF roles (authoring, implementation, adversarial review) are performed by Claude and Claude sub-agents.
